data.narrative:
Saronic is a Series D defense and security company founded by Dino Mavrookas, Rob Lehman, Vibhav Altekar, and Doug Lambert, currently valued at $9.25B post-money and having raised $2.6B+. The relevant comparables universe consists of three same-stage, same-sector private peers (Chaos Industries at $4.5B, Epirus at $1.4B, and Onebrief at $1.1B), alongside a broader Defense & Security comp set of eight private firms and two public companies (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS).

Two valuation methods are applicable to Saronic. Stage-progression analysis, applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $9.2B base, implies a valuation of $13.9B. Peer-median methodology across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables yields a median valuation of $1.4B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $4.5B. The stark divergence between these approaches reflects substantial heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the outsized valuation of Chaos Industries relative to Epirus and Onebrief.

Triangulation of both methods produces a median valuation of $7.6B, bounded by a low of $1.1B and a high of $16.0B. The current $9.25B valuation sits above the peer median but below stage-progression guidance, suggesting Saronic commands a premium relative to peer comparables. Key uncertainties center on whether Saronic's commercial traction and market positioning justify this premium, and whether the heterogeneity within the peer set reflects genuine business differences or measurement error.
